evals.prompt: I want to forecast 2m temperature and 10m wind speed 3 days ahead from 2024-01-15 00Z. I have an A100 80GB GPU. Write me the full inference script.
evals.expected_output: Selects a suitable medium-range prognostic model, a compatible data source, calculates nsteps, and generates a complete deterministic inference script. Output should closely match the reference target file.
assertions: Selects a medium-range (MR class) prognostic model appropriate for 3-day forecasts
Presents data source options and selects one compatible with the chosen model
Correctly calculates nsteps based on model time step and 3-day horizon
Generated script uses earth2studio.run.deterministic with correct arguments
Script includes output_coords filtering to t2m, u10m, and v10m variables (both wind components needed for wind speed)
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_1_target.py

evals.prompt: Write me a deterministic forecast script using Pangu weather model with GFS data source, initialized on 2024-06-01 00Z, running 5 days out. Save output as Zarr. I only want to save z500 and t850.
evals.expected_output: Generates a script using Pangu model, GFS data source, ZarrBackend IO, 5-day forecast with correct nsteps, filtering output to z500 and t850.
assertions: Uses Pangu model class (from earth2studio.models.px)
Uses GFS data source (from earth2studio.data)
Uses ZarrBackend for IO
Correctly calculates nsteps for 5 days with Pangu's time step
Includes output_coords with only z500 and t850
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_2_target.py

evals.prompt: I need a 7-day global forecast of geopotential height at 500 hPa and mean sea level pressure. I have a 48GB GPU. What model should I use and what's the best data source? Generate the full script.
evals.expected_output: Selects a global MR model that fits in 48GB VRAM, picks a compatible data source, calculates nsteps for 7 days, and generates the complete script with z500 and msl output.
assertions: Selects a model with Rec VRAM <= 48GB
Presents at least two model options with tradeoff explanation
Selects a compatible data source verified via lexicon
Correctly calculates nsteps for 7-day horizon
Script includes output_coords filtering to z500 and msl
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_3_target.py

evals.prompt: Generate a deterministic forecast script using GraphCast with ARCO ERA5 data. Initialize from 2023-09-01 00Z and run for 10 days. Save all output variables to Zarr.
evals.expected_output: Generates a script using GraphCastOperational model, ARCO data source, ZarrBackend, 10-day run. Does not filter output_coords (saves all model outputs).
assertions: Uses GraphCastOperational or GraphCast model class
Uses ARCO data source
Uses ZarrBackend for IO
Correctly calculates nsteps for 10 days with GraphCast's time step
Does not include output_coords filtering (saves all variables)
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_4_target.py

evals.prompt: I want to produce a 24-hour precipitation forecast over North America starting from the latest available data. I have an H100 GPU. What regional model and data source should I use? Write the script.
evals.expected_output: Selects a nowcasting (NWC) or regional model suitable for NA 24h forecasts (e.g. StormCast), pairs with HRRR data, calculates nsteps for 24 hours, generates script with precipitation output.
assertions: Selects a regional or NWC model with NA coverage
Pairs the model with HRRR or another NA-specific data source
Presents at least two options and explains regional vs global tradeoff
Correctly calculates nsteps for 24-hour horizon with model's time step
Script targets precipitation variable(s) in output
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_5_target.py

evals.prompt: I want to run a 30-day forecast of global 2m temperature and sea surface temperature. I need a model suited for subseasonal timescales. Write the full inference script.
evals.expected_output: Selects DLESyM as the appropriate S2S/CM model for 30-day forecasts. Pairs with a compatible data source (ERA5 via ARCO or CDS), calculates nsteps for 30 days, and generates the complete script.
assertions: Selects DLESyM model for the subseasonal/seasonal timescale
Uses a compatible data source verified via lexicon
Correctly calculates nsteps for 30-day horizon with DLESyM's time step
Generated script uses earth2studio.run.deterministic with correct arguments
Script includes output_coords filtering to t2m and sst or equivalent variables
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_6_target.py

evals.prompt: I want to build a deterministic forecast workflow from scratch without using earth2studio.run.deterministic. Write a complete script that manually fetches initial conditions, creates the model iterator, steps through it, and writes each step to the IO backend. Use Pangu for a 5-day forecast of z500 and t2m from 2024-03-01 00Z with GFS data and ZarrBackend output.
evals.expected_output: Generates a complete script that reimplements the deterministic workflow loop manually: fetches data with fetch_data, sets up IO coordinates, creates the prognostic iterator, loops through steps writing output via split_coords, and uses map_coords for coordinate subsetting. Does NOT call earth2studio.run.deterministic.
assertions: Does NOT import or call earth2studio.run.deterministic
Uses fetch_data from earth2studio.data to fetch initial conditions onto the device
Calls prognostic.create_iterator(x, coords) to create the model iterator
Iterates through the model with a for/while loop, breaking at nsteps
Uses map_coords to subselect output variables/coordinates at each step
Uses split_coords before writing to the IO backend
Correctly sets up IO total_coords with time and lead_time arrays before the loop
Calls io.add_array to initialize the IO backend before writing
Correctly calculates nsteps=20 for 5 days with Pangu's 6h time step
Generated script closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_7_target.py
